description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a dual-wing configuration, featuring a prominent red color on both the upper and lower wings as well as the tail surfaces. The fuselage is a tan or beige color, streamlined and cylindrical, with a visible propeller mounted at the nose. The structure supporting the wings and tail is composed of yellow struts, giving the aircraft a classic, open-frame appearance. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, indicating a taildragger configuration. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be open, suitable for a single pilot.
description: A vintage red biplane with a tan fuselage, open cockpit, and yellow struts, featuring dual wings and classic taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red dual wings, tan fuselage, yellow struts, and the open cockpit near the front.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the biplane's tail structure, red tail surfaces, and the alignment of the dual wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propeller, open cockpit, and the classic biplane wing arrangement.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the propeller, open cockpit, dual red wings, and the main landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers forming a standard cruciform arrangement.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a rear skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the propeller, likely single-seat with no visible canopy.
